notes: Pico with WiFi/BLE, one of the most cost-effective networking solutions

onboard_peripherals.name: WiFi chip (CYW43439)
onboard_peripherals.notes: WiFi/BLE module connected via internal SPI. GPIO 23/24/25 are not available to the user

onboard_peripherals.name: LED
onboard_peripherals.notes: The onboard LED is controlled by the WiFi chip (not RP2040 GPIO). Control via `from machine import Pin; Pin('LED', Pin.OUT)`, does not occupy RP2040 GPIO

notes: RP2040 peripherals are fixed to GPIO pins. Each function can only be selected from the set of pins listed in pin_options
GPIO 23/24/25 are occupied by the WiFi chip (CYW43439) and completely unavailable
GPIO 29 has a special purpose (VSYS voltage monitoring or ADC), generally not used for peripherals
The onboard LED is controlled internally by the WiFi chip, use `machine.Pin('LED', ...)` to operate, does not occupy RP2040 pins
I2C0 (GPIO4/5) is the default I2C, be careful not to conflict with UART1 (TX=4/RX=5)
For WiFi-related code, use `import network; wlan = network.WLAN(network.STA_IF)` instead of the ESP32 approach
